title: Databases and SQL
description: The relational model of tables and keys, normalising a flat-file design to 1NF/2NF/3NF, and the core SQL statements used to query and update relational data.
objectives: Describe a relational database as a set of tables (relations) made up of rows (records) and columns (attributes/fields)
Identify primary keys and foreign keys, and explain how a foreign key creates a relationship between two tables
Explain the redundancy and update/insertion/deletion anomalies caused by an unnormalised flat-file design
Normalise an unnormalised table to first, second and third normal form (1NF, 2NF, 3NF)
Write SQL SELECT statements using FROM, WHERE and ORDER BY to retrieve and sort data
Write SQL INSERT INTO, UPDATE and DELETE statements, and a simple INNER JOIN across two tables using a foreign key

lessons.title: The relational model: tables, keys and relationships
Querying and updating data with SQL
